threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube; locals just say 'the London Underground' like it's a posh family member.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the entire bus with old soul classics. By the time we hit Stockwell, we were all singing along like it was karaoke.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street performer with a pet parrot serenading the buskers on the corner of Weser and Hasenheide. It's all about the unexpected.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous film screenings in courtyards, complete with popcorn and neighbors debating the plot with their dogs.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to order a burrito without rice and still get the extra salsa on the side. Tourists just gawk at the menu.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo hop off the 19 while clutching a bouquet, then get chased by a pigeon. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think we spend all day at the park. Most locals just grab a coffee and rush back to work, the parks are for that peaceful moment, not all-day lounging.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot 'em by their struggle with the metro ticket machines – they’re the ones pressing buttons like it’s a video game.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when a bus zooms by without stopping, not knowing it’s just how things roll in Hackney.
Last Saturday on the 36, a guy tried to convince everyone he was the next big thing in grime. Only his tracks were from 2005. The driver just shook his head.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw someone using a shopping cart as a makeshift grill in Körnerpark. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a currywurst stand next to a high-end vegan café, with patrons debating art and politics over late-night snacks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to check if the BART's closed before heading to that late-night taco spot. Tourists won't see the signs until they’re stranded.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a jazz trio set up on the 49 bus. They started playing as the doors closed at Market, and it felt like a flash mob in slow motion.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all hipster cafes and boutiques, but many of us still rely on the old-school mercats for daily groceries. Can't get that vibe in a store!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their confusion in the Mercat de Gràcia; they still ask vendors for prices in euros instead of just grabbing what catches their eye.
